data.title: Bitcoin Whales Move Billions Amid U.S. Crypto Legislation Push, Sparking Market Jitters
data.description: Key Takeaways:A dormant Satoshi-era Bitcoin whale moved $9.6 billion after 14 years, triggering market correction concerns.Whales may be reacting to U.S. regulatory developments, including the GENIUS Act.Despite fears, Bitcoin ETFs continue to see strong inflows, totaling $522 million on Thursday.U.S. Senate passes three major crypto bills, boosting regulatory clarity but raising compliance questions.Whale Movements Shake Market as Crypto Bills Advance in U.S. SenateA series of massive Bitcoin transfers by long-dormant whale wallets is raising alarm across the crypto market, just as the U.S. Senate passed three key cryptocurrency-related bills aimed at increasing regulatory oversight.On-chain analysts tracked the movement of a Satoshi-era whale, who transferred approximately $9.6 billion worth of Bitcoin — the first time the wallet was active in 14 years. The transaction triggered fears of a potential sell-off, with analysts suggesting that regulatory pressures may be influencing whale behavior.GENIUS Act Raises Concerns Among Large HoldersOne potential trigger is the GENIUS Act, recently passed as part of broader crypto legislation. The bill requires stablecoin audits and could introduce more stringent compliance rules for crypto-related financial services. Financial analyst Jacob King warned that the legislation could "burst the biggest bubble and fraud in financial history," referring to Bitcoin itself.The suggestion that whales may preemptively offload holdings in response to increasing oversight has fueled concerns of near-term volatility.However, others downplay the threat. Analysts from Nansen argue that long-term whales are generally resilient to regulation and are likely unfazed by political developments.ETF Inflows Remain Strong Despite VolatilityDespite the whale activity and regulatory uncertainty, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs have recorded 11 consecutive days of net inflows, drawing over $522 million in new capital on Thursday alone. This suggests that institutional demand for Bitcoin remains robust, even as market sentiment wavers.ETF inflows are often seen as a barometer of longer-term confidence, indicating that traditional investors may be looking past short-term volatility tied to whale movements or government actions.U.S. Senate Clears Path for Crypto OversightMeanwhile, the U.S. Senate passed three major crypto bills this week, signaling growing momentum for comprehensive digital asset regulation. These bills aim to define clearer legal frameworks around stablecoins, exchange operations, and digital asset classification — all of which could reshape how institutions interact with crypto in the coming years.While the legislation improves clarity, it also raises new questions around compliance, privacy, and decentralization — factors that may influence whale behavior and retail investor sentiment alike.

data.title: Charles Schwab to Launch Spot Trading Services for Bitcoin and Ethereum
data.description: Charles Schwab is preparing to offer direct spot trading services for Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH), according to CEO Rick Wurster, signaling deeper institutional involvement in crypto markets.

data.title: Julius Baer Economist Warns US Dollar May Keep Falling Amid Policy Instability and Fiscal Strain
data.description: Key Takeaways:The U.S. dollar has declined 15% since its September 2022 peak.Julius Baer’s David A. Meier blames policy volatility and rising fiscal pressure.Analysts say the dollar's safe-haven appeal may be weakening as the U.S. itself becomes a source of global risk.The U.S. dollar could remain under pressure in the coming months due to growing policy instability and worsening fiscal conditions, according to David A. Meier, economist at Julius Baer Group.“The recent depreciation trend may continue if U.S. policymakers fail to stabilize the fiscal outlook,” Meier said, noting that the dollar has already fallen about 15% since peaking in September 2022.While the greenback has traditionally served as a global safe haven during periods of uncertainty, Meier suggests that this role could be challenged when the source of risk originates from the U.S. itself.“It's not clear whether the dollar’s status as a safe haven is breaking down, but we often see dollar weakness during episodes when risk aversion stems from U.S. policies,” he added.Restoring market confidence in the dollar would require a reduction in policy uncertainty and clearer long-term fiscal planning.The commentary comes amid rising market volatility and renewed investor focus on sovereign debt sustainability in major economies.

data.title: SEC Considers “Innovation Exemption” to Boost Asset Tokenization After Stablecoin Act Passes
data.description: Key Points:SEC Chair Paul Atkins is exploring an “innovation exemption” to support asset tokenization.The move follows the U.S. House passing the Stablecoin Act, signaling growing support for blockchain-based finance.The exemption could pave the way for new trading models and infrastructure for tokenized securities.The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is evaluating a potential “innovation exemption” aimed at accelerating the adoption of asset tokenization and blockchain-based financial infrastructure, according to SEC Chairman Paul Atkins.Speaking after the U.S. House of Representatives passed the Stablecoin Act, Atkins said the Commission is reviewing how to update its policies within the existing regulatory framework to encourage innovation without compromising investor protections.“This exemption could help promote new trading methods and support the development of infrastructure for tokenized securities,” Atkins said, signaling a more open stance toward blockchain-powered financial instruments.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Shows Neutral Funding Rates
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that the funding rates of major centralized and decentralized exchanges (CEX and DEX) are currently reflecting a neutral market stance. The specific funding rates for mainstream cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain balance between contract prices and the prices of underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging this fee, to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring contract prices remain close to the asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% signifies a baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, a funding rate below 0.005% suggests a bearish market sentiment.
